title: Western Kenya Peace Initiative (WKPI)
tagline: UCDP is a proud partner of the Western Kenya Peace Initiative — a coalition of 12 organisations working together to promote peace and stability across western Kenya.
intro_p1: Peace is the foundation on which all development is built. Without it, communities cannot thrive, children cannot learn, and families cannot flourish. UCDP is a committed partner of the Western Kenya Peace Initiative (WKPI) — a powerful coalition of 12 organisations working together to promote peace, reduce conflict, and build social cohesion across the western parts of Kenya.
intro_p2: The WKPI brings together civil society organisations, community groups, and development actors who share a common commitment to peaceful coexistence in the region. Together, the coalition addresses the root causes of conflict — including inter-community tensions, resource-based disputes, and ethnic divisions — through dialogue, mediation, community engagement, and peace education.
intro_p3: As a WKPI partner, UCDP contributes its community presence, networks, and expertise in working with vulnerable populations — particularly street youth and children — to the coalition's broader peace-building work. We believe that children and youth who are supported, educated, and empowered are less likely to become victims or perpetrators of conflict.

steps.title: Coalition Meetings
steps.text: WKPI partners meet regularly to coordinate peace-building activities, share intelligence on conflict hotspots, and plan joint interventions.

steps.title: Community Dialogue
steps.text: The coalition facilitates community dialogues, bringing together groups in conflict to find common ground and peaceful resolution.

steps.title: Peace Education
steps.text: Partners conduct peace education sessions in schools, communities, and youth groups to build a culture of non-violence and dialogue.

steps.title: UCDP's Contribution
steps.text: UCDP contributes its community networks, expertise in youth and child welfare, and ground-level presence to the coalition's peace-building work.
